opener_head: The Year the Sofa Kings Ran Out of Competition
opener_body_1: Six seasons in, we finally got a wire-to-wire champion. The Sofa Kings drafted well, traded better, and spent fourteen weeks reminding everyone about both. It was the most complete season anyone has put together in this league, and it was almost impossible to enjoy.
opener_body_2: Behind them, chaos. Four teams finished 7–7. The playoff cut came down to points-for on the final Sunday. And at the very bottom, the Turf Titans authored the worst season in league history — a 2–12 campaign that began with the second overall pick and ended with a group chat apology.
closer: Draft night is the last Saturday in August. The Titans pick first. Bring cash, bring receipts, and bring a better roster than last year.
footer_line: Lawson Family & Friends Fantasy League · Season VI in the books

timelines.event: Sofa Kings open with 152. Nobody takes it seriously yet.
The 118–118 tie. A missed extra point. A month of arguing.
The Bijan trade. The Fumble Fam’s season effectively ends.
Turf Titans score 58.1 and start a group chat apology tour.
Four teams finish 7–7. The cut line comes down to points-for.
Sofa Kings 141, Blitz Brigade 119. Wire to wire.
